subject: [RFC PATCH 02/40] mm: page_alloc: per-cpu pageblock buddy allocator

2026-05-20.patch_summary: This patch introduces a per-cpu pageblock buddy allocator to improve scalability and reduce contention on large machines. The current zone->lock bottleneck is addressed by extending the per-CPU page cache (PCP) to operate on whole pageblocks, eliminating affinity violations and reducing lock hold times. The PCP refill process is divided into four phases: recovering owned fragments, claiming whole pageblocks, grabbing sub-pageblock chunks, and traditional __rmqueue() with migratetype fallback. This approach enables PCP-level merging, reduces fragmentation, and allows the allocator to degrade gracefully when necessary.
